title: Guya — Ram Dass, Myth of Sisyphus, words-as-waves
html:
TL;DR

  • Early-November 2021 note, freshman OSU term: a four-line philosophy stack — "Ram Dass / The Myth of Sisyphus / Focus, and things will start to make sense / I think there is a deduction of communication. Words can be deduced down to feelings or waves. This can be a powerful and profitable concept. Just very difficult to make it tangible."
  • First and only corpus reference to Ram Dass (Be Here Now) and to Camus's Myth of Sisyphus — the two reading anchors Yahir is logging as he ramps the year-one OSU intellectual stretch.
  • The words-as-waves / deduction-of-communication line is the most ambitious abstract claim in the 2021 batch: Yahir framing language as compressible to feeling-as-signal and flagging its commercial potential — proto-AI/embedding intuition six years before he gets near LLMs.
  • "Focus, and things will start to make sense" — bare aphorism, classic manifesto style note register stapled between the reading list and the speculative concept.
  • "Guya" (the title) — unresolved; possibly mishear of "Goya," a person, or a placeholder header. Adds to the file's puzzle quality.

Source quote

"Ram das

The myth of sisiphus

Focus, and things will start to make sense

I think there is a deduction of communication. Words can be deduced down to feelings or waves. This can be a powerful and profitable concept. Just very difficult to make it tangible"

Open questions

  • Did Yahir actually read Ram Dass's Be Here Now and Camus's Myth of Sisyphus, or are these aspirational reading-list captures?
  • The words-as-feelings/waves idea — does it later resurface in the 2024-2026 AI / embedding notes? If yes, this is a six-year-old intuition that aged well.
  • "Guya" — typo, person name, or coined term? Single mention, ambiguous; potential-entity candidate.
